What happens when two players have the same amount of goals/assists/mom's/rating?
In these cases, the prize is awarded to the player with fewer minutes played. In all cases, only one prize will be awarded.

title: 22. National Cup
content:
Each country will have its own national cup competition. Of course, the number of rounds in the cup will depend on the number of teams in that country. We'll present you with a relation between the number of teams in a country, and the number of rounds/teams the national cup will have:
10 Teams - 3 Rounds - 8 Teams in the National Cup
50 Teams - 5 Rounds - 32 Teams in the National Cup
210 Teams - 7 Rounds - 128 Teams in the National Cup
850 Teams - 9 Rounds - 512 Teams in the National Cup
3,410 Teams - 11 Rounds – 2,048 Teams in the National Cup
13,650 Teams - 13 Rounds – 8,192 Teams in the National Cup

As you can see in almost all cases, some teams won't be able to participate in the cup. To get the necessary number of teams of a country where all teams are not able to participate in the cup we use a ranking system (explained in Section 17).

Another important thing to be mentioned is concerning cup gate receipts. 60% of the gate receipts of cup matches will be given to the home team, and 40% to the away team. If we're talking about the final, the receipts will be shared (50%/ 50%).

Cup matches are special matches. If the game is not settled after 90' it will go into extra-time. If the team's are still tied after 120' then there will be a penalty shoot-out. The winning team will move on to the next round.

As told before, cup matches are played mid-week on Mondays or Tuesdays (depending on your country's match schedule). A team cannot play a friendly and a cup match on the same week – as these are played on the same day. During off-season, teams might be required to play cup-ties during the weekends. This will only happen in countries with 5 divisions or more.

Players train after cup matches. However, only those who have played at least 30 minutes in the game will train. For more information about training and cup matches please read Section 8.

Section 7 will talk about player experience gained during Cup matches.
Read about Cup Matches and suspensions in Section 14.

The cup winner will be given a monetary prize of € 800,000. The runner-up will earn € 400,000.

title: 23. Friendly Matches & Instant Matches
content:
Your team can play a friendly match once a week, if it's not set to play in the national cup (on Mondays or Tuesdays). During off-season, teams can also schedule friendly matches on weekends. Off-season friendly matches have increased attendances. This was made to compensate the losses teams have for not playing official matches (especially important for countries that do not play cup matches during the off-season).
Friendly match gate receipts will be shared between both clubs (50%/ 50%).

Players train after friendly matches. However, only those who have played at least 30 minutes in the game will actually train. For more information about training and friendly matches please read Section 8.

Experience gained in friendly matches is discussed in Section 7.
Read about Friendly Matches and suspensions in Section 14.

PM Fans have a feature called “Friendly Pool”: they will have the opportunity to subscribe to the "Friendly Pool" for a nominal cost which will allow them to be automatically setup with another team a few hours before the friendly match is to be played.

Each Wednesday after 2:00 AM (server-time) the subscriptions will be opened. Once you subscribe, and pay the small cost (5000 €) your team will be added to the weekly friendly pool.
You'll still be able to schedule friendly matches in a normal way (receive/send invitations). However, if you schedule a friendly then your team will be taken out of the pool and your money will be refunded.
Each Sunday, at 2.30 AM (server-time), the draw takes place: all the teams in the friendly pool will be randomly assigned to play a friendly match against another participating team. If the number of participating teams is odd then one of the teams will be left out of the draw - this team's owner will receive a message, have the cost refunded, and still have over a whole day (in most cases) to schedule a friendly match manually.
Instant Friendly Games (IM’s)


Some important aspects of this feature:
- You can only set up a match for the next hour, with 15m breaks. There are limits to the number of matches than can be created on each bracket, depending on the server(s) activity at the time. The max. number of matches per bracket is 15. Once the match is generated, managers will receive a notification at the Match Center and the live match starts immediately.
- PM Supporters can play up to 7 matches per week. Non PM Supporters can play 3 matches per week. You can buy extra matches for 100 PM Coins!
- There are no injuries, suspensions, experience gains, training, fitness losses and/or player/team update of any kind. In fact injured players can play these games.
- There will be ranking that will split all managers between leagues (World class, Professional, Semi-professional, Amateur and Not Ranked).
- You can set the weather for the match. If you want you can also invite a specific team for a match. If you don’t want to invite a team just leave the "Team ID" field at "0".
- You can watch Instant Matches Live.
- The PM Match Viewer is free for all users in these kind of games.

About the ranking, as said, the ranking is divided in 4 leagues:
- World Class league
- Professional league
- Semi-professional league
- Amateur League

Each league may have many series based on the number of managers participating in the Instant Matches. The exception is the World class league that only has 1 series, which means that only the best managers will be on the world class league. Each series will have 22 managers and the ranking is updated every Monday.

During the Monday weekly update of the ranking, the following tasks will be done:
- The top eleven managers of the Professional League will be promoted to the World class league;
- The worst eleven managers of the World class league will be relegated to the Professional league;
- The promotions and relegation of the remaining leagues will be dynamic, depending on the number of ranked managers;

The percentage of managers in each league are:
- World Class: 22 managers
- Professional League: 15% of all ranked managers
- Semi-Professional League: 35% of all ranked managers
- Amateur League: 50% of all ranked managers

Managers get ranking points when they win and draw. They lose points if they lose games. There might be bonuses depending on the fact that you’re playing against a team with higher ranking.

You can find these new pages at the Competitions main menu, at the "Instant Matches" option.

title: 24. PM Cups
content:
PM Cups can either be created by the administration of the game (official cups) or by normal users. Official cups usually give rewards to the best 4 teams. These rewards may be PM Supporter or PM Coins. From time to time we can offer other prizes as well.

These cups have the following characteristics:
- The cups may have two possible formats: A group qualifying stage with a late knockout stage, or straight knockout stage.
- The date and time of games, as well as the number of games per week, is different for each cup, for example there may be cups with 4 games per week. A cup can have several matches per day but these time slots must be shared between all match days.
- Players do not lose fitness in cup games.
- Players do not gain experience or train in cup games.
- There are no attendances for clubs as well.
- The cup creator has 15 days to manually initiate the draw. Otherwise it will just be initiated automatically.

All users can participate in these cups. The PM Fan requirement drops but it’s replaced by PM Coins. The new cost structure follows below:
- In order to enter a cup, non PM Fans will have to pay 200 PM Coins. PM Fans don’t have to pay anything. Some official cups might require a cost for everyone though.
- All users can create Cups but they have a creation cost though. For Cups with less than 100 teams the cost is 400 PM Coins. You will have to spend 500 PM Coins if you set up a cup for more than 100 teams but less than 200. If you want to create a cup for more than 200 teams, then you will have to spend 600 PM Coins. Cup creators do not have to pay signup fees in their own cup.

title: 25. International Team Competitions
content:
International competitions will take place during the same time as tournament games (Fridays at 13:00 server time), and teams playing in these competitions will be automatically dropped from their tournaments (they will lose every missing tournament game by walk-over). Injuries and attendance income will also be following tournament rules. The only difference is that all games will give some experience points to the players; experience points in the final knock-out phases of the CWL and WCL will be doubled, and will also be doubled in the last four rounds of the WCC. It will be possible to play Match of the Season games in international competitions, but these will be deducted from the club's available MOTS: meaning the “two per season” maximum still applies. The final match will always be played on a neutral, grassy-pitched stadium.

An international team's ranking exists, similar to the national teams ranking, called... international club ranking. The position of a country on this ranking will define the number of teams from that country that will be qualified to the new international competitions. The WCL and CWL group phases start at the 3rd week of the season (1st week with league matches) and the WCC on the 4th week.

So, the new competitions are:

World Champions League (WCL)
All of the top league champions from all PM countries will be qualified to play in this competition.
The competition will have a group stage, and a final knock-out stage. This will be modeled almost in the same fashion as the World Cup qualifying and final stages. In the group stage all 60 teams will play; the only difference to the World Cup qualifiers is that there will only be 1 round of games (the teams will play against each other only once). All of the group winners, plus the 7 best 2nd placed teams (a total of 16 teams) will qualify to the knock-out stage, exactly like in the World Cup.
The winner prizes for the finalists will be: 600000€ to the World Champion; 400000€ to the second place team.

Cup Winners League (CWL)
Same rules as WCL, except the teams taking place in this one will be the cup winners from all countries. In case the cup winner also won the top league, they will take part in the WCL instead, and the other cup finalist will be qualified to the CWL.
The winner prizes for the finalists will be: 200000€ to the CWL winner; 100000€ to the second-placed team. The reason for the low prizes here is mainly that the national cups already have a higher prize compared to the top league champion prize.

World Club Cup (WCC)
This will be a knock-out competition only, national-cup-style, with no group stages. 256 teams will be qualified to play in this cup, and they will be chosen as follows:
- 9 teams from the top 8 countries according to the new club ranking (2nd to 6th placed from the top division plus the 4 B division winners; in case any of these is qualified to the CWL cup the 7th placed from the top division is qualified instead of it).
- 5 teams from the next 12 countries on the ranking (2nd to 6th placed from the top division, 7th placed if any of the others takes part on the CWL).
- 4 teams from the next 16 countries (2nd to 5th from top div., 6th if any of the others are unavailable);
- 3 teams from the next 12 countries (2nd to 4th from top div., 5th if any of the others is unavailable);
- 2 teams from the bottom 12 countries (2nd and 3rd from top div., 4th if any of the others is unavailable).
The lowest ranked team will always play at home.
The winner prizes for the finalists will be: 600000€ to the WCC winner; 400000€ to the second-placed team.

title: 26. National Federations & National Teams
content:
National Federation


Just like in real-life, there are National Federation (and Presidents) in PManager. Their task is to appoint CTs for National Teams, change the national teams' kits and initiate referendums for changing the federal rules. There will be a national federation for each country. Each federation has a president, elected by the national users.
Here is some info about this feature:

The election of the president works like this:
- You can vote only concerning the federation of your country;
- You can vote on any national user that has come forward as a candidate;
- You cannot vote for yourself.
- In case of a draw in an election, the user that applied to the position first will be settled as winner

(where applicable, these last items are valid both for National Federation and PIF elections)

Users, after the election starts, will need to officially state they’re candidates so other users can vote for them. There’s a period of 3 days for candidates to present themselves before the real election starts. The candidate with more votes after the election period of 7 days is the new president.

The president of the National Federation will be the user responsible for nominating the National Team managers of the country (both the main NT & U21 managers). The president can name whoever he wants, of any nationality, except he can not nominate himself; a president can’t ever be national manager at the same time. The user nominated for the manager job will still need to accept the proposal, of course. The president will also be responsible for the firing of the National Managers, and will be able to do it anytime he wishes.

Other presidential tasks include:
- All presidents will decide amongst them who will host the next World Cups (using an internal voting system);
- Presidents will be able to choose the stadium used for NT games: the national stadium;
- Presidents have the ability to change the name of the first division of the country by creating a national referendum in which all users from the country can vote;
- Presidents can change the name of the national federation, also subject to national referendum.
- Presidents can set the national team kits.
- Presidents can set the national URL, the federation logo and a lot of other minor options.
- Presidents can send national announcements to the community.
- Presidents can nominate vice-presidents: these have all the options the president has except creating referendums, firing/nominating National Managers and voting for the World Cup site. They can’t be National Managers either.

National referendums are open for 7 days and can only be made once a month (for each type of referendum).

A president is elected "for life"; there’s only two ways to get him off the presidential position. One of the ways is simple: he quits the job himself. The other is through a "revolution". A revolution works like this: Every election, the president is elected with a certain number of votes. After he’s elected, people can choose either to support the current president or to support a revolution (in the federation page). If the number of users supporting a revolution is greater than 75% of the votes the president got on the last election, a revolution takes place and a new election is started. If the number of votes he got elected with exceeds the number of users in the country, the number of users will be applied instead (so, if 75% of the total number of users are against the president, a revolution will occur).
During an election the president cannot use any of his powers. If the election was started because he resigned, nobody will be able to vote for him (he won’t be able to set himself up as a candidate). In case of a revolution, he can take part as a candidate if he wishes to do so.

This will obviously deeply change the current NT managers system, but will hopefully work more sensibly and give the users more power over who is in charge of their national teams.
This will be also be an important base for a lot of planned features. New referendums will be added to the federation’s list of actions as these new features are developed and laucnhed. In the future, our plan is to give the federation alot of powers concerning specific country-based rules.
National Teams


Just like in real-life, there are National Teams (NTs) in PManager. In fact, there are two NTs per country: the main NT and the U21 NT. The cool thing about National Teams, besides representing your own community, is that they are managed by human managers. They are appointed by Presidents of National Federations.

Every two seasons, the World Cup (WC) occurs. As the WC and the U21 WC are not played during the same season, this means that, at the end of every season there's a World Cup: either the WC or the U21 WC.

How does a World Cup work?
The first phase after the elections are over is the “friendly phase”. For 8 straight weeks, managers will have the possibility to build their squad in friendly matches. It's usually during these matches that managers test the abilities of their NTs so they can prepare for the Qualifying Stage (QS).

The Qualifying Stage happens in 10 matches with groups of 6 teams (home and away matches). The top NT will qualify for the World Cup and then, depending on the number of groups, the best 6 second placed NTs will qualify as well. These groups are seeded based on the National Team ranking (explained further down this section). The seed method is quite easy to understand: NTs are divided into pots of 6 teams depending on their ranking (first 6 in pot 1, next 6 in pot 2, etc); then, the draw will pick one team for each pot in order to make all groups. This will ensure that the top NTs won't have to face each other in the Qualifying stage in order to make things more interesting and balanced.

After the QS is completed, the draw to the WC is made. 16 teams will be qualified for the knock-out stage. The first round of draw will try to avoid clashes between the first placed on all groups. After the first round of the draw is played, all of the next 3 rounds are seeded randomly. As you're already aware at this time, there are 4 elimination rounds in the WC. The winner of the final match can then be considered the best NT of the game!

When are World Cups played?
At the beginning of each season, right after the new season's transition.

What's the difference between main NTs and U21 NTs?
The difference is obvious, for all of you who are familiar with International Football. U21 teams can only have players with 21 years old or less. Main NTs can have players from all ages.

Then why do I see U21 teams with players over 21 years old?
That's a special case, and only happens during the U21 World Cup – knock-out stage. As players age at the season transition, it would be extremely unfair to “lock” players that were 21 years old in the previous season who started the U21 campaign as 20 year olds. So, U21 National Team managers will be able to have these players in their squad during the WC only. However, and this is very important, they have to make sure that they are in the team before the new season transition occurs. They won't be able to call them up again once they are 22. So they must make sure that they are in their NT squads for the final group stage match in order to be available for the knock-out stage.

So, the time needed to play all Friendly Matches, Qualifying and World Cup is 2 regular seasons?
Yes, that's it.

What are the benefits of having players in the National Teams?
Your players will get extra experience, and (usually) international players are sold for higher prices on the transfer market. Those are the only in-game advantages for International Players. Getting players on an NT is also very prestigious for the owners of those players.

Do players get injured in National Teams matches?
Yes, they do.

Can NT managers use MOTS in their matches?
Yes, they can. A National Team has the possibility to use 2 MOTS in the friendly + qualifying stage. MOTS can't be used in the final stage of the World Cup. They can use PIC whenever they want; however, the experience gained while using PIC is less than if you played normal or MOTS.

What are the most important things NT managers should know?
Besides the basic (tactics, ME, etc) they should know that:
- National teams have a temporary squad that will later be released in order to form a definitive squad.
- The definitive squad cannot be released more than 6 days before the match.
- Only 22 players (max) can be selected for a definitive squad. The min. is 16 players.
- After the definitive squad is released, managers have access to all tactical options.
- Injured & suspended players can be called up; however, they obviously cannot play.
- Players cannot be in the main and U21 squad at the same time. If you don't see the option to call up a player then it is probably because he's already on the other squad. There should be coordination between the main and U21 manager regarding the players that can be “shared” between squads.
- National Team matches are played on Fridays at 20:00 (server time). World Cup matches are played as follows:
- Eight-Finals are played on the first Wednesday of the next season;
- Quarter-Finals are played on the first Friday;
- Semi-Finals are played on the second Wednesday;
- The Final is played on the second Friday. All matches are 20:00 server time.
- The definitive squad announced for the World Cup (5 days or less before the first match) cannot be changed until the end of the World Cup.
- International Team matches can only be played on grassy pitches.
- Free players can be called up to National temporary squads, but they won't be able to take part in the definitive squad once you release it.

How does the ranking for the NTs work?
Each international match will generate ranking points for both U21 and NTs. The ranking will work as follows:
>> Points for result:
- Win (no penalty shootout) 3
- Draw 1
- Loss (no penalty shootout) 0
- Win (penalty shootout) 2
- Loss (penalty shootout) 1

>> The value will then be multiplied as follows:
- Game type: Friendly match: x1 | WC Qualifier: x2 | WC Stage: x4
- Opponent’s position ranking with the following formula, where:
¤¤¤¤ X = Number of Countries in PM | Y = Opponent’s Ranking Position
¤¤¤¤ Multiplier = (X - (Y - 1)) / (X / 2) (This multiplier cannot be lower than 0.5)

So, the amount of ranking points if you play and win an NT Friendly against the first ranked team:
>> 3 (winning points) x 1 (game type mult.) x 2 (ranking mult.) = 6 points
In a World Cup final stage >> 3 x 4 (game type mult.) x 2 = 24 points

This version is actually very similar to the formula used by FIFA. We have removed some parts in order to add simplicity.

The ranking will work on an NT season basis (an NT season is made up of 2 regular seasons). This ranking will be reset once the World Cup finishes (remember that NT's seasons are only increased once a WC is completed). However, the points are saved until 2 seasons in the past, and will be used to define the final ranking position.

An example:
- The main English NT wins 40 points in season 1, 30 points in season 2 and 50 in season 3. Their ranking points are the sum of these 3 seasons (120). Then, in season 4 they get 80 points. Season 1 points are dropped, and the ranking points are updated to 30+50+80=160.

title: 27. PM Coins & PM Fan
content:
You can now exchange your PM Coins into PM Fan days. This will be the only way to buy PM Fan from now on. PM Coins will allow us to buy different kinds of services that will be introduced in PM very soon.

To exchange your PM Coins into PM Fan days just go to Shop > PM Coins.
As said, with PM Coins you can buy PM Fan! PM Fan is a service that adds some extra features to the free version of game. This package is meant to add great fun and enjoyment to the users that subscribe to it.

Our main goal is always to offer the same possibilities of success for everyone, and so we've decided that these extra features shouldn't give any direct advantages to their subscribers. However, with PM Fan we can guarantee that you'll have a deeper experience of what PManager really is!

The reason for the creation of this service is to basically help us with financial support so that we can continue to develop and enhance the game further. It will also help us cope with the high maintenance costs of the game servers.

You'll find more information about what PM Fan is and what it offers you by going to Shop >> What Is It? - on the top menu.
Free Offers


This section can be found at Shop >> Free Offers. We have re-organized the section where you can get PM Fan for free and introduced new partners: SponsorPay, SuperRewards. Check it our as there should some nice freebies waiting for your activation! You can complete multiple tasks and be rewarded with PM Coins for free. Many of the tasks do not involve a credit card or purchase of any kind. Once an offer is completed, the offer will be added to your account after verification. For support, click "Offer Status" on each partner.

title: 28. Community
content:
Our aim is to have a big, active and interesting community. You can count on us to provide you with tools to improve and expand the community, but it's really up to you to make it happen. You have our word that we'll be actively present on the game forums, reading all of the important stuff you write to us. Be aware that you can't really expect us to answer everything you write as it is almost impossible due to time issues. Although, we would love to do it!

We have internal forums that you can use to communicate with all of the other users. The forums work by subscription mode: for you to have access to a certain forum, you must subscribe to it. The first page after you click on Community >> Forums is the configuration page. You can take a look on your pre-defined forums and subscribe to as many as you wish.

You should, however, remember that we have some rules that you must respect if you want to take an active part in our user community. We'll talk about them in the next section of this manual.

title: 29. Behavior
content:
As we told before, if you want to be part of our user community then you really have to respect some rules. These rules apply not only to the forums, but also to every area where you can interact with other users. These other areas are: blogs, internal messages, etc.

What is not allowed:
- Advertising players (except on the ads forum, created for that exact purpose)
- Requesting friendly matches (except on the ads forum).
- Multiple posting of similar comments or threads on the forum (or blog entries), or posting comments not related to the topic (spam).
- Bumping threads on the forum (spam).
- Making any kind of attack on a person, race, nation or religion.
- Starting political discussions.
- Excessive use of swear words and/or insults.
- Excessive use of CAPITAL letters.
- Making cheat accusations (use the proper cheat report form by going to Help >> Report Cheaters).
- Advertising things or services you want to buy/sell in real life.
- Supporting or inciting to the use of illegal software.
- Promoting the use of illegal substances or criminal activities.
- Discussing decisions taken by members of the Administration of the game.
- Creating blog entries, forum threads or posting anything linking to adult/obscene material (porn, etc.).

Exceptions (at forums):
- Only the national team manager can place ads in National Forums about players that belong to the National Team who are currently for sale. If the player belongs to the National Manager's personal team, he is not allowed to do it.
-Sending private messages asking for votes on PIF / National Federations is not allowed and can result in immediate suspension from the election.

Punishments :
- All infractions to these rules may lead to punishments coming from the administration staff. The punishments are decided based on the severity of the infraction. Punishments are also based on the previous history of any infractions by that user. The punishments may vary. They consist of fines, bans from the forums for some time or even the instant termination of your PManager account.
- The forum moderators may move, delete or close topics without notice and/or explanations as they see fit.
- All decisions taken by the administration team and the punishments made cannot be discussed in any public location within the game.
These rules can change without prior notification from the staff.

Please check our Terms of Service

Please check our Shop Refund Policy

title: 30. Manager's Certificate & Mentorship System
content:
The manager's certificate is a tool that newbies are prompted to use and complete in order to achieve two goals:
- A quick way to learn about the game once they start playing it;
- The community's recognition. Managers without the certificate can't be considered top managers by the rest of the community.

This tool is also very important to the game as it allows you to become eligible to manage a National Team or to become a mentor.

The Certificate is made up of a series of questions about the game as well as some actions you need to complete. It should be quite easy to use!

All those who are able to complete the Certificate will receive a nice surprise at the end!
Mentorship System


With our users help and with this mentorship system we hope to reduce the learning curve new users face themselves with when they start the game.

Here are some infos:
- Mentors are now marked as inactive in case they miss activity regarding their apprentices.
- Mentors are warned whenever they get are set with a new apprentice.
- Apprentices from inactive mentors are now automatically re-assigned to new (active) mentors.
- Mentors can only get 10 apprentices max. per language instead of 40.
- Counting all apprentices in all languages, a mentor can only have 30 max.
- From now on mentors can drop their apprentices out (useful for example if they are not active or you don’t speak the same language).

There are now rewards for mentors in case they are able to integrate their apprentices in the game successfully. Here are the rewards we are giving:
>> Apprentice has been active in the game for 3 months: You get 7 days of free PM Fan.
>> Apprentice has been active in the game for 6 months: You get 8 days of free PM Fan.
>> Apprentice has been active in the game for 12 months: You get 9 days of free PM Fan.
>> Apprentice has been promoted to a higher division: You get 5 days of free PM Fan.
>> Apprentice has bought his own PM Fan: You get 5 days of free PM Fan.
(the length of these bonus might be changed in the future if we find it appropriate)

Regarding these rewards:
- Rewards will only be given in cases the apprentices are registered after 1/09/2009 (1st of September, including). Also, in order to claim a reward, apprentices must have made a login in the last 10 days. They also need to be linked to you as an apprentice for the same period the reward is about. For example, if we’re talking about a 3 months-active award, if the apprentice registration date was 5 months ago and the user is only your apprentice for 2 months you won’t be able to claim the reward until the next month.
- You need to claim rewards in order to get them as they are not assigned automatically. In order to do that you just have to open your apprentice details and click on the "Reward" link.
- You can opt to give your rewards to the apprentice. There’s an option at the Rewards section for that.
- After claiming the reward you will have to wait a couple of days as it will be manually validated by our staff. We have decided to use a manual verification process to prevent abuse. This also means that we can actually reject a reward if we conclude that the mentor did nothing to help the apprentice (we have the necessary tools to analyze the communications made between both users).

title: 31. PMDD
content:
PMDD, abbreviation of Planetarium Manager Developer Depot, consists of programs created to assist and aggregate all the developers who successfully create external tools to support Planetarium Manager users managing their teams.

XML feeds are used to access PM data.

If you are interested in developing such a tool, please create a PMDD ticket by going to Help >> Contact Us then submit a ticket describing your interest.
We will give you access to a private forum where you can discuss with other PM developers about your tool, and also obtain support on how to use the XML feeds.

title: 32. Game Administration
content:
From time to time, the Administration team of PManager appoints regular users as official team members. These user's jobs help us maintain the game, and allow you (a normal user) to have direct contact with someone both close with the administration and with your local community. We have four different staff roles:

Administrator (ADM) are the creators and maintainers of the game. They are responsible for the game's programming and design/development of new features. They are also responsible for fixing any bugs/errors. They are also responsible for the nomination of new staff members. We have splited the "ADM" role we had in the game in two new roles:

Developers (DEV) - The main task of these users is to fix bugs and develop new features.

Community Manager (CM) - These kind of users are in charge of collecting all kind of feedback from the community and bring it to the Developers and those that are in charge of running the game.

Super Moderator (SM) - They are usually responsible for managing all of the issues regarding their local communities (unless they are global SM's who are responsible for all issues regarding every single local community in general). Their main functions are: dealing with cheaters and cheating reports, validating new user accounts and answering emails. They also recommend new ADM's, MOD's and LA's for their local communities.

Moderators (MOD) - Their main function is to moderate our game forums. They are also responsible for the moderation of any other public form of communication inside the game (except private messages which, of course, are not public).

Language Administrator (LA) - Their only job is to keep the language version they are managing up-to-date. They also translate game announcements to their language.

You should know that we fully support our team members. Specifically talking about our SM's, they have all the necessary tools to analyze cheating suspects so we'll backup all their decisions. You should contact us through our support center if you want to make any comments regarding our staff's decisions. Bragging about them on the forums won't really help much as you'll be breaking our rules of behavior.

You should also know that our SM's do not need to explain full details on how they came to the conclusion that someone cheated. If they did that they'd be ruining our cheater-catching system by telling cheaters how to break/go around it. The game is ruled by our rules and if you don't agree with them then it is better for you to not play the game at all. You should also remember that you agree with the rules every time you login into your account, and are bound to comply with them.

All of our staff members (except ADM's) work on a purely voluntary basis. Keep that in mind before judging them unfairly: they're only trying to help us and their local communities by offering their free leisure time.

You can know who these members are by looking at Help >> Administration on the top menu.

The only official way to communicate with the Game Administration is to create a ticket at our support center available at Help >> Contact us (top menu). You will be able to create support tickets that will be answered by our staff, usually in a period of 0 to 7 days. Please avoid creating several tickets with the same content, and be aware that every time you update your ticket by adding a new comment, the ticket will be moved to the bottom of the queue.

PManager also has an internal communication system for blocked users (called the Cheating Support System). All this means is that we do not support email support for any reason whatsoever. Sending an email to any of our available email addresses will not be answered unless we ask you directly to do so.

title: 33. PManager Week
content:
PManager is played in real-time, so each day of the week we'll have several different things happening. (All times are server-time)

Monday
- Stats database updates at 5:30
- Friendly / Cup matches in some countries (check the events page on the top Help Menu)
- Trainings after those matches

Tuesday
- Inactive user verification at 3:30
- Bosman player transfers at 10:00
- Friendly / Cup matches in some countries (check the events page on the top Help Menu)
- Trainings after those matches

Wednesday
- Stats database updates at 5:30
- League matches in some countries (check the events page on the top Help Menu)
- Trainings after those matches

Thursday
- Free player transfers at 12:00
- League matches in some countries (check the events page on the top Help Menu)
- Trainings after those matches

Friday
- Financial updates at 8:00
- Bosman transfers at 10:00
- Tournament matches at 13:00
- International Teams Competition matches at 13:00
- National Team matches at 20:00
- Tournament awards and next round draws at 15:00
- International Team Competition awards and next round draws at 15:00

Saturday
- Stats database updates at 5:30
- League matches in some countries (check the events page on the top Help Menu)
- Trainings after those matches

Sunday
- Friendly Pool match assignments at 2:30
- League matches in some countries (check the events page on the top Help Menu)
- Trainings after those matches

Updates that run on a daily basis:
- Injury updates at 5:30
- Teams assignments to new users at 6:00
- Regular transfers run every single minute

Other updates worth mentioning
- Cup awards are run on Wednesdays at 1:30
- Elections end on Sundays at 14.00 and on Fridays at the same time (for temporary elections only)
- Season transition is run during the night on the Monday after the last round of the league

These times are described using PM's server-time (Portugal/Lisbon time, normally GMT+0). Eventually, there might be some delays during these updates, so please be patient if delays do occur.
